title: For a binary search tree, if its pre-order travel sequence is {

title: Given two algorithms A1 and A2, with time complexities being
content: Given two algorithms $$A_1$$ and $$A_2$$, with time complexities being $$T_1 = O(n^2)$$ and $$T_2 = O(n\log n)$$, respectively.  Then $$A_2$$ must be faster than $$A_1$$.
author: 陈越
description: Given two algorithms $$A_1$$ and $$A_2$$, with time complexities being $$T_1 = O(n^2)$$ and $$T_2 = O(n\log n)$$, respectively.  Then $$A_2$$ must be faster than $$A_1$$.

title: In an undirected acyclic graph G with 4 connected components, th

C. 94

title: We represent a directed graph as an adjacency list:
content:
We represent a directed graph as an adjacency list:
```
v0: -> null
v1: -> v0 -> v3 -> null
v2: -> v1 -> null
v3: -> null
v4: -> v1 -> v6 -> null
v5: -> v2 -> v4 -> null
v6: -> null
v7: -> v1 -> v2 -> null
```
Which one below is a topological order of the given graph?
author: 陈越
description:
We represent a directed graph as an adjacency list:
```
v0: -> null
v1: -> v0 -> v3 -> null
v2: -> v1 -> null
v3: -> null
v4: -> v1 -> v6 -> null
v5: -> v2 -> v4 -> null
v6: -> null
v7: -> v1 -> v2 -> null
```
Which one below is a topological order of the given graph?

title: The array representation of the disjoint sets is given by S = {
content: The array representation of the disjoint sets is given by S = { 4, 1, 1, -4, 9, 10, -1, 9, -5, 8 }. Keep in mind that the elements are numbered from 1 to 10. After invoking Union(Find(2), Find(10)) with union-by-size and path compression, which of the following statements is FALSE?
author: 陈越
description: The array representation of the disjoint sets is given by S = { 4, 1, 1, -4, 9, 10, -1, 9, -5, 8 }. Keep in mind that the elements are numbered from 1 to 10. After invoking Union(Find(2), Find(10)) with union-by-size and path compression, which of the following statements is FALSE?

title: Suppose that the level-order traversal sequence of a max-heap is
content: Suppose that the level-order traversal sequence of a max-heap is { 82, 65, 17, 26, 8, 12, 3 }. Use the linear algorithm to adjust this max-heap into a min-heap, and then call DeleteMin. The preorder traversal sequence of the resulting tree is:
author: 陈越
description: Suppose that the level-order traversal sequence of a max-heap is { 82, 65, 17, 26, 8, 12, 3 }. Use the linear algorithm to adjust this max-heap into a min-heap, and then call DeleteMin. The preorder traversal sequence of the resulting tree is:

choices: The length of the shortest path from `v2` to `v0` must be no greater than 11.
The length of the shortest path from `v2` to `v0` must be no less than 11.
The length of the shortest path from `v2` to `v0` must be less than 15.
The length of the shortest path from `v2` to `v0` must be greater than 15.

title: In a weighted undirected graph, if the length of the shortest pa
content:
In a weighted undirected graph, if the length of the shortest path from `v1` to `v0` is 13, and there exists an edge of weight 2 between `v2` and `v1`, then which one of the following is correct?
@[](3)

A. The length of the shortest path from `v2` to `v0` must be no greater than 11.
B. The length of the shortest path from `v2` to `v0` must be no less than 11.
C. The length of the shortest path from `v2` to `v0` must be less than 15.
D. The length of the shortest path from `v2` to `v0` must be greater than 15.
author: 陈翔
description: In a weighted undirected graph, if the length of the shortest path from `v1` to `v0` is 13, and there exists an edge of weight 2 between `v2` and `v1`, then which one of the following is correct?

title: Consider a special queue which is a linear structure that allows
content: Consider a special queue which is a linear structure that allows insertions at one end, yet deletions at both ends.  Given an insertion sequence { 42, 36, 21, 63, 16 }.  Which of the following is NOT a possible deletion sequence?

title: In a directed graph, the relation between the sums of in-degrees

title: Push and pop { 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 } sequentially into then out
content: Push and pop { 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 } sequentially into then out of a stack. Suppose that each number is pushed into a queue right after it gets out of the stack, which of the following dequeue sequences is possible?  **Note: making 4 consecutive pops are NOT allowed in this stack.**

title: Function Call Simulation with Linked Stack
content:
这是程序填空题模板。
在这里填写题目要求，例如：本题目要求打印“Hello World!”。

在代码中设置填空并填写正确答案，选择一段代码，点击上方编辑器的 **[添加填空]** 按钮或 **[添加多行填空]** 按钮即可成功添加填空，可在下方设置填空答案及分数。

[添加填空]：![add-blank-inline.png](https://static.pintia.cn/assets/problems/add-blank-inline.png)
[添加多行填空]：![add-blank-block.png](https://static.pintia.cn/assets/problems/add-blank-block.png)

样例程序：
```c++
#include <stdio.h>

int main()
{
  printf("");

}
```
上方是一段具有两个代码填空的程序代码，点击 **[复制内容]** 按钮（所见即所得），或选中全部代码后点击 **[生成评测代码]** 按钮（Markdown），即可得到一段用于评测的代码，并填入下方评测代码中使用。
 [生成评测代码]: ![file-conversion.png](https://static.pintia.cn/assets/problems/file-conversion.png)
***注：***创建题目后需自行设置与评测代码对应的测试数据*（默认无）*
author: 李松
